response.justification: This candidate is a strong general software engineer with excellent fundamentals in TDD, clean architecture, and proven ability to build complex systems at scale. However, they lack the core technical requirements for an enterprise IDE/JVM role: no Spring ecosystem experience, no Kotlin, and minimal Java. Their sole IDE experience is C-focused (National Instruments LabWindows), not JVM-focused. Ramp-up time would be substantial despite their engineering capabilities.
strengths: Excellent software craftsmanship fundamentals demonstrated through TDD practices, clean architecture principles, and multiple open-source contributions
Proven track record with complex systems at massive scale (millions of requests/operations across multiple roles)
Full-stack engineering capability and demonstrated ability to learn diverse technology stacks rapidly
concerns: No Spring ecosystem experience despite being a core requirement for enterprise Java development
No Kotlin in skill set and minimal Java experience—primary focus has been TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and C/C++
IDE experience exists but is C-language focused (LabWindows/CVI); no evidence of JVM tooling, language services, or enterprise framework internals

key_factors.factor: No Kotlin or Deep Java Background
